# POST-08 PSNR reference fixture

This directory holds the committed reference MP4 that
`crates/encoder/tests/psnr_regression.rs` compares against on every CI
run (Phase B of the two-phase procedure defined in
`.planning/phases/02-cinematic-post-production-export/02-11-PLAN.md`).

## Artifacts

- `../1min_reference_1080p30.mp4` — the committed reference. ~5–10 MB,
  1080p, 30 fps, H.264 CRF 18, 60-second duration. Generated by the
  `generate-psnr-reference` binary from the shared graph builder in
  `crates/encoder/src/export/reference_graph.rs::build_reference_graph`.

## Two-phase procedure

**Phase A — one-time seeding.** Run once on a known-good build, typically
when Plan 02-11 first lands or whenever the filter graph intentionally
changes (e.g. a Plan-01/05/06/07/08/09 filter_complex snapshot was
re-accepted). The generator renders the reference graph at 1080p30 and
writes the output to the canonical path:

```bash
cargo run --package encoder --bin generate-psnr-reference --release -- \
    --output crates/encoder/tests/fixtures/1min_reference_1080p30.mp4 \
    --ffmpeg "$(which ffmpeg)"
git add crates/encoder/tests/fixtures/1min_reference_1080p30.mp4
git commit -m "[fixture](02-11): seed POST-08 PSNR reference 1080p30"
```

The commit-message **MUST** start with the literal prefix `[fixture]`. The
`reference_fixture_committed_with_prefix` meta-test enforces this via
`git log -1 --format=%s -- <path>` — any other prefix fails the build
(T-02-35 integrity / audit-trail mitigation).

**Phase B — every CI run.** `cargo test -p encoder --test psnr_regression`
re-renders the reference graph at 1080p30 into a temp candidate MP4 and
calls `compute_psnr(ffmpeg, reference, candidate)` (FFmpeg `psnr`
filter). The test asserts `average >= 38.0` dB. A drop below 38 dB is
treated as a pipeline regression and the build fails.

## Intentional filter-graph changes

When a downstream plan intentionally updates the filter graph (for
example, Plan 02-08 tightens the cursor-overlay easing), the committed
reference is expected to mismatch until it is regenerated. The workflow
is:

1. Ship the filter-graph change with its insta snapshot update (each of
   Plans 02-01/05/06/07/08/09 owns its own `filter_complex.snap`).
2. Re-run Phase A to regenerate `1min_reference_1080p30.mp4`.
3. Recommit the fixture with a `[fixture]` commit-message prefix
   explaining the deliberate change, e.g.
   `[fixture](02-08): re-seed PSNR after cursor easing update`.

This enforces an auditable paper trail for every reference-fixture
change, so a reviewer can trace each pixel-level regression bump to a
specific filter-graph decision.

## Skip semantics (local development)

The PSNR regression test is **not** `#[ignore]`-gated any more — once
the fixture is committed, every `cargo test` runs it. However, when:

- the fixture is absent on disk (fresh clone before Phase A has run), **or**
- `ffmpeg` is not available on `$FFMPEG_BIN` or `$PATH`

the test prints a `skip:` message (with the exact regeneration command)
and returns early. This is the same gate pattern used by the Plan 01-08
`tests/probe.rs` + `tests/pipeline.rs` integration tests when the
per-triple FFmpeg artifact is absent.
